edges.description: Beta-catenin signaling cooperates with YAP1-dependent transcription
Beta-catenin activity reinforces a fetal liver-like differentiation state
Cooperative transcription sustains immature hepatic identity
Joint pathway activation drives tumor cell expansion
Immature hepatic identity is accompanied by oncofetal marker expression
The aggressive oncofetal state is coupled to NFE2L2 pathway activation
The oncofetal C2 transcriptional state is associated with more aggressive tumor behavior
Stress adaptation supports survival and growth of aggressive tumor cells

nodes.description: Nuclear beta-catenin and YAP1 co-occur in most hepatoblastomas and act as a cooperative transcriptional module required for robust tumor formation.

evidence.reference_title: Activation of β-catenin and Yap1 in human hepatoblastoma and induction of hepatocarcinogenesis in mice.
evidence.snippet: We observed nuclear localization of β-catenin and Yap1 in 79% of hepatoblastoma samples but not in most hepatocellular carcinoma or intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma samples.

evidence.reference_title: Activation of β-catenin and Yap1 in human hepatoblastoma and induction of hepatocarcinogenesis in mice.
evidence.snippet: Overexpression of constitutively active forms of Yap1 and β-catenin in mouse liver led to rapid tumorigenesis, with 100% mortality by 11 weeks.

evidence.reference_title: Activation of β-catenin and Yap1 in human hepatoblastoma and induction of hepatocarcinogenesis in mice.
evidence.snippet: Knockdown of Yap1 or β-catenin in hepatoblastoma cells reduced proliferation in an additive manner.

nodes.description: Hepatoblastoma growth depends on beta-catenin/YAP1-driven proliferative output, which can be attenuated experimentally by pathway inhibition.

evidence.reference_title: Activation of β-catenin and Yap1 in human hepatoblastoma and induction of hepatocarcinogenesis in mice.
evidence.snippet: Knockdown of Yap1 or β-catenin in hepatoblastoma cells reduced proliferation in an additive manner.

evidence.reference_title: mTOR inhibition affects Yap1-β-catenin-induced hepatoblastoma growth and development.
evidence.snippet: Rapamycin treatment decreased HB burden, almost normalizing liver weight to body weight ratio.

nodes.description: Somatic CTNNB1 mutations or deletions are the dominant recurrent driver in hepatoblastoma and lead to persistent nuclear beta-catenin activity.

evidence.reference_title: Exploration of CTNNB1 ctDNA as a putative biomarker for hepatoblastoma.
evidence.snippet: Driver mutations in the CTNNB1 gene (encoding β-catenin) are a hallmark of sporadic hepatoblastoma (HBL).

evidence.reference_title: A combined clinical and biological risk classification improves prediction of outcome in hepatoblastoma patients.
evidence.snippet: Mutations of CTNNB1, NFE2L2 and TERT were found in 135 (78%), 10 (6%) and 10 (6%) patients, respectively, and the adverse C2 subtype of the 16-gene signature in 63 (36%) patients.

nodes.description: Hepatoblastoma retains hepatic progenitor and fetal liver programs rather than completing normal hepatocytic differentiation, aligning the tumor with an immature developmental state.

evidence.reference_title: Genomic analysis of hepatoblastoma identifies distinct molecular and prognostic subgroups.
evidence.snippet: Our analysis of 88 clinically annotated HBs revealed three risk-stratifying molecular subtypes that are characterized by differential activation of hepatic progenitor cell markers and metabolic pathways:

nodes.description: A subset of hepatoblastomas acquires NFE2L2-driven stress adaptation that tracks with non-fetal histology, vessel-invasive growth, and worse outcome.

evidence.reference_title: Genomic analysis of hepatoblastoma identifies distinct molecular and prognostic subgroups.
evidence.snippet: high-risk tumors were characterized by up-regulated nuclear factor, erythroid 2-like 2 activity; high lin-28 homolog B, high mobility group AT-hook 2, spalt-like transcription factor 4, and alpha-fetoprotein expression; and high coordinated expression of oncofetal proteins and stem-cell markers, while low-risk tumors had low lin-28 homolog B and lethal-7 expression and high hepatic nuclear factor 1 alpha activity.

evidence.reference_title: A combined clinical and biological risk classification improves prediction of outcome in hepatoblastoma patients.
evidence.snippet: Patients carrying a NFE2L2 mutation had a significantly worse 3-year OS (57% versus 88%) than NFE2L2 wild-type patients and were more likely to have vessel invasive growth and non-fetal histology.

nodes.description: Aggressive hepatoblastomas express oncofetal and stem-cell-associated markers including LIN28B, HMGA2, SALL4, and AFP, linking fetal identity to high-risk tumor biology.

evidence.reference_title: Genomic analysis of hepatoblastoma identifies distinct molecular and prognostic subgroups.
evidence.snippet: high-risk tumors were characterized by up-regulated nuclear factor, erythroid 2-like 2 activity; high lin-28 homolog B, high mobility group AT-hook 2, spalt-like transcription factor 4, and alpha-fetoprotein expression; and high coordinated expression of oncofetal proteins and stem-cell markers, while low-risk tumors had low lin-28 homolog B and lethal-7 expression and high hepatic nuclear factor 1 alpha activity.
